---
title: "Title of the Empirical Working Paper"
shorttitle: "Short Title"
nber_number: ""
author:
  - name: "First Author"
    affiliation: "Department of Economics, University Name"
    email: "first.author@example.edu"
  - name: "Second Author"
    affiliation: "Business School, University Name"
    email: "second.author@example.edu"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %Y')`"
thanks_title: "We thank seminar participants and colleagues for helpful comments. All errors are our own."
acknowledgements: "Replace this text with grant acknowledgements, data-use acknowledgements, and replication-package notes."
disclaimer: "The views expressed herein are those of the authors and do not necessarily reflect the views of any affiliated institution."
output:
  pdf_document:
    template: template.tex
    latex_engine: pdflatex
    keep_tex: true
    number_sections: true
    highlight: null
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center",
  fig.width = 6.5,
  fig.height = 3.7,
  dpi = 300,
  out.width = "0.85\\linewidth"
)

# Daily empirical workflow packages. These are loaded only if installed.
load_if_available <- function(pkg) {
  if (requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(pkg, character.only = TRUE))
    TRUE
  } else {
    message("Package not installed: ", pkg)
    FALSE
  }
}

pkgs <- c(
  "data.table", "ggplot2", "fixest", "modelsummary", "kableExtra",
  "broom", "sandwich", "lmtest", "causalmetrics"
)
invisible(vapply(pkgs, load_if_available, logical(1)))

if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {
  ggplot2::theme_set(ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 11))
}
```

\begin{abstract}
This template is designed for empirical economics and marketing-science working papers. The abstract should state the research question, research design, data, main quantitative result, and contribution in one concise paragraph.

\bigskip
\noindent \textbf{JEL:} C21, C93, I12

\noindent \textbf{Keywords:} causal inference, randomized trials, empirical economics
\end{abstract}

\clearpage
\setcounter{page}{1}

# Introduction

State the research question, empirical setting, source of identifying variation, and main result. A useful first paragraph answers four questions: What is the economic question? Why does it matter? What variation identifies the effect? What is the headline estimate?

This template uses `natbib`, so citations can be written directly in LaTeX as `\citet{lin2013agnostic}` or `\citep{angrist1996identification}`. Bibliography commands are also written directly in LaTeX at the end of this file rather than placed in the YAML header.

# Data and Institutional Background

Describe the raw data, sample construction, unit of observation, outcomes, treatment variables, timing, and important institutional details.

```{r demo-data}
if (requireNamespace("data.table", quietly = TRUE)) {
  set.seed(123)
  dt <- data.table::data.table(
    unit_id = 1:500,
    cluster = sample(1:50, 500, replace = TRUE),
    treat = rbinom(500, 1, 0.5),
    x1 = rnorm(500),
    x2 = rbinom(500, 1, 0.4)
  )
  dt[, y := 1.5 * treat + 0.6 * x1 - 0.3 * x2 + rnorm(.N)]
  head(dt)
}
```

# Research Design

Define the estimand and identifying assumption. For a randomized trial, define assignment $Z_i$, treatment receipt $D_i$, outcome $Y_i$, covariates $X_i$, and the assignment or clustering level.

\begin{assumption}[Random assignment]
Conditional on pre-treatment covariates $X_i$, assignment is independent of potential outcomes:
\[
Z_i \indep \{Y_i(1), Y_i(0)\} \mid X_i.
\]
\end{assumption}

The baseline estimating equation is
\[
Y_i = \alpha + \tau Z_i + X_i'\beta + \varepsilon_i.
\]

# Empirical Workflow Algorithm

Use the algorithm environment for procedures that readers may want to replicate.

\begin{algorithm}[H]
\caption{Baseline empirical workflow}
\label{alg:workflow}
\begin{algorithmic}[1]
\Require Raw data $D$, outcome $Y$, assignment $Z$, covariates $X$, cluster variable $G$
\State Construct the analysis sample and document all exclusions.
\State Report summary statistics and baseline balance.
\State Estimate the intention-to-treat effect of $Z$ on $Y$.
\State If compliance is imperfect, report the first stage of $Z$ on treatment receipt $D$.
\State Choose robust or clustered standard errors based on the assignment mechanism.
\State Report robustness checks and threats to validity.
\Ensure Tables, figures, and a replication-ready data pipeline.
\end{algorithmic}
\end{algorithm}

# Main Results

Use direct estimator calls here. The template should not hide regressions behind wrappers.

```{r demo-regression, results='asis'}
if (requireNamespace("fixest", quietly = TRUE) && exists("dt")) {
  fit <- fixest::feols(y ~ treat + x1 + x2, data = dt, cluster = ~cluster)

  if (requireNamespace("modelsummary", quietly = TRUE)) {
    modelsummary::modelsummary(
      list("Outcome" = fit),
      output = "latex",
      stars = TRUE,
      gof_omit = "IC|Log|Adj|Within"
    )
  } else {
    print(summary(fit))
  }
}
```

# Figures

Figures should have informative captions and notes.

```{r demo-figure, fig.cap='Simulated treatment and control outcome distributions'}
if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE) && exists("dt")) {
  ggplot2::ggplot(dt, ggplot2::aes(x = y, fill = factor(treat))) +
    ggplot2::geom_density(alpha = 0.35) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      x = "Outcome",
      y = "Density",
      fill = "Treatment",
      caption = "Notes: Replace this demo figure with a project-specific plot."
    )
}
```

# Robustness and Validity

Discuss balance, attrition, compliance, clustering, functional form, alternative outcomes, multiple testing, and external validity.

# Conclusion

Summarize the main estimate, interpretation, mechanism, limitations, and contribution.

\clearpage
\appendix
\counterwithin{figure}{section}
\counterwithin{table}{section}

# Appendix: Additional Tables and Figures

Put long tables, additional figures, alternative specifications, and derivations here.

\clearpage
\singlespacing
\bibliographystyle{ecta}
\bibliography{references}
